label.purpose: Counters, secondary labels, and status text.

shadows.name: canvas-lift
shadows.purpose: Ambient separation for the drawing canvas only.

motion.name: state-standard
motion.purpose: Fast product feedback for hover, active, and reveal states.

components.name: Neutral Button
components.description: Default rectangular command button for toolbar and stroke controls.
components.css: .ds-btn-neutral { height: 38px; min-width: 38px; padding: 0 12px; border: 1px solid #d8d1c4; background: #fbfaf6; color: #161616; border-radius: 0; font: 500 14px Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-btn-neutral:hover { border-color: #0f766e; } .ds-btn-neutral:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #0f766e; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Primary Playback Button
components.description: Active state button for playback, loop, and selected tool modes.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { height: 38px; min-width: 38px; padding: 0 12px; border: 1px solid #0f766e; background: #0f766e; color: #fffefa; border-radius: 0; font: 500 14px Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #0b5f59; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #0f766e; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Danger Button
components.description: Destructive action button that stays structurally quiet.
components.css: .ds-btn-danger { height: 38px; min-width: 38px; padding: 0 12px; border: 1px solid #d8d1c4; background: #fbfaf6; color: #a13a2f; border-radius: 0; font: 500 14px Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-btn-danger:hover { border-color: #a13a2f; } .ds-btn-danger:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #a13a2f; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Stroke Control Card
components.description: Compact per-stroke control surface with color swatch, metadata, actions, and progress.
components.html: Stroke 142 pts
components.css: .ds-stroke-card { display: grid; gap: 10px; padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #d8d1c4; background: #fffefa; color: #161616; border-radius: 0; font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-stroke-title { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; gap: 10px; } .ds-stroke-name { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; font-weight: 650; } .ds-swatch { width: 18px; height: 18px; border: 1px solid rgba(0,0,0,.15); background: linear-gradient(90deg, #ff4d4d, #2563eb); } .ds-meta { color: #6d6a61; font-size: 13px; } .ds-stroke-actions { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(3, minmax(0, 1fr)); gap: 8px; } .ds-stroke-actions button { height: 38px; border: 1px solid #d8d1c4; background: #fbfaf6; color: #161616; } .ds-progress { height: 6px; overflow: hidden; background: #e9e3d7; } .ds-progress span { display: block; width: 65%; height: 100%; background: #0f766e; }

components.name: Speed Panel
components.description: Progressive disclosure range control for playback speed.
components.html: 1x
components.css: .ds-speed-panel { display: grid; grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1fr) 44px; align-items: center; gap: 10px; padding: 8px 10px; border: 1px solid #d8d1c4; background: #fbfaf6; } .ds-speed-panel input { width: 100%; accent-color: #0f766e; } .ds-speed-panel span { color: #6d6a61; font: 500 13px Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; text-align: right; }

narrative.overview: Stroke Replay is a product UI for artists and designers, not a marketing surface. It should feel like a quiet gallery workbench where line, rhythm, and playback are treated as material. The interface stays restrained so the drawn stroke remains the hero, but the composition must not collapse into ordinary whiteboard blandness.
keyCharacteristics: Restrained product density with gallery-like calm.
A single kinetic accent for action, motion, and selected state.
Canvas-first layout: controls frame the work, never compete with it.
Hard-edged UI surfaces that feel precise rather than playful.
Progressive disclosure for advanced controls like speed and gradient stops.

rules.name: The Stroke-Owns-Color Rule
rules.body: Saturated color belongs primarily to the artwork and active states. Do not turn the interface into a rainbow because gradient brushes exist.

rules.name: The Teal Means Motion Rule
rules.body: Kinetic Teal is reserved for actions and playback state. If everything is teal, nothing is active.

rules.name: The Product Sans Rule
rules.body: Use one well-tuned sans family across the tool. Do not introduce display fonts into buttons, labels, counters, or stroke controls.

rules.name: The Flat Controls Rule
rules.body: Buttons, stroke cards, panels, and global controls are flat at rest. Use borders and state color before adding shadows.

dos: Do keep the canvas visually dominant; the interface frames the work.
Do use Kinetic Teal for playback, active mode, progress, and selected controls.
Do keep controls dense but grouped, so the tool feels professional rather than sparse.
Do reveal advanced controls progressively, as with speed sliders and gradient stops.
Do preserve baseline accessibility: readable contrast, visible focus, reduced-motion respect, and non-color cues for critical state.
donts: Don't make it feel like a generic whiteboard.
Don't make it feel like a basic browser paint demo.
Don't make it dull, office-like, or without artistic presence.
Don't turn gradient brush color into decorative interface color.
Don't add Photoshop-level complexity or dense professional-suite chrome before the core stroke workflow earns it.
